testBaseline.note: Baseline from Sprint 8 end (4/4 stories). 1766 passed + 1 skipped + 19 deselected. Pre-existing 4 ruff errors in src/server/ai/ollama.py and tests/test_remote_ollama.py — may be resolved as a side effect of US-CMP-005 work, but not required. Session 19 did not modify src/ (only dev scripts, fixtures, docs).

sprintNotes: Recommended execution order: start with US-CMP-007 (backup receiver, no deps) and US-162 (baseline calibration, no deps). US-CMP-005 is blocked on Spool prompt templates — see offices/tuner/inbox/2026-04-16-from-marcus-ai-prompt-templates.md. If templates land during sprint, pick up US-CMP-005, then US-CMP-006 and US-163 unblock. If Spool does not deliver, file a blocker and ship the 2 unblocked stories.
US-162 builds on scripts/report.py; keep additions additive and preserve existing --drive/--trends/--last behavior.
No changes to src/pi/ or src/common/ except where explicitly called out.

stories.title: Backup receiver endpoint
stories.intent: Accept multipart file uploads from the Pi (database snapshot, logs, config) so the Pi's data has a disaster-recovery copy on the server. This is the server-side counterpart to future Pi-side backup push (US-150, B-037 scope — not this sprint).

groundingRefs.unit: backups retained per type per device

acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
POST /api/v1/backup accepts multipart/form-data with fields: file (binary), type (one of: database|logs|config), deviceId
Authenticated via US-CMP-002 X-API-Key dependency
Rejects extensions outside {.db, .log, .json, .gz} with 415
Rejects files larger than MAX_BACKUP_SIZE_MB (default 100) with 413
Rejects unknown 'type' values with 422
Stores at {BACKUP_DIR}/{deviceId}/{type}/{stem}-{ISO8601 timestamp}.{ext}
BACKUP_DIR is settings-driven with default ./data/backups — creates directories as needed
Rotation: after successful write, keeps only the most recent BACKUP_RETENTION_COUNT (default 30) files per (deviceId, type) pair
Rotation invariant: never deletes the last remaining file even if older than the retention policy would allow
Returns 200 with JSON {status: 'ok', path, bytes, rotated: N}
Returns 500 with a clean error if the destination path is unwritable
invariants: Idempotent filename handling: re-uploading the same file name produces a new timestamped copy, never overwrites
No changes to sync or analyze endpoints
stopConditions: If filesystem permissions on BACKUP_DIR default prevent creation, STOP and propose a safer default or ask CIO

stories.title: Baseline calibration tooling
stories.intent: Once real drives are flowing (post-BT pairing), the sim-derived thresholds will drift from the Eclipse's actual steady-state values. Give the CIO a CLI that proposes updates based on real data, requires explicit approval to apply, and never mutates live thresholds silently. Can be built and unit-tested on sim data before real drives exist.

groundingRefs.unit: minimum real drives before --calibrate shows proposals

acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
scripts/report.py --calibrate computes proposed baseline updates from drive data and prints a comparison table (sim baseline | real baseline | delta | action)
Proposals use the analytics in src/server/analytics/basic.py for per-parameter steady-state values across all real drives for the filtered device
--calibrate does NOT modify any stored baselines — it is read-only
Output table lists all parameters with meaningful divergence (|delta| > configurable threshold, default 2%)
--calibrate --apply writes the approved baseline values to a new 'baselines' table (new SQLAlchemy model in src/server/db/models.py)
--apply requires --calibrate to have been run in the same invocation (not a separate cached proposal) — compute and write atomically
When fewer than 5 real drives exist for the device, --calibrate prints a clear message 'Need N more real drives before calibration is meaningful' and exits 0
'Real' drives are identified by a new column or convention — grooming Q: mark via profile_id='real' or a new is_real boolean. Propose both options in the pre-flight audit.
Test coverage: proposal math, insufficient-data path, apply-without-calibrate rejection, idempotent re-apply
invariants: --apply is the only mutating path and it writes only to the new baselines table
Existing --drive and --trends behavior of scripts/report.py unchanged
stopConditions: If the 'real vs simulated drive' distinction is not resolvable without schema change, STOP and propose both options with tradeoffs in the pre-flight audit

stories.title: Real AI analysis endpoint
stories.intent: Replace the US-147 stub with a real Ollama-backed analyzer. Pulls drive data from MariaDB, renders a prompt from Spool's templates, calls Ollama /api/chat, stores ranked recommendations. This is the first story where the system tells the CIO something actionable — keep the failure modes graceful so a dead Ollama or an empty time window does not crash sync flow.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
POST /api/v1/analyze with {drive_id, parameters} returns real recommendations from Ollama, not the US-147 stub
Response shape preserves the US-147 forward-compat contract: {status, analysis_id, message, recommendations[], model, processingTimeMs}
recommendations[] entries have: rank (int, 1-indexed), category (string), recommendation (string), confidence (float 0.0-1.0)
System message loaded from src/server/services/prompts/system_message.txt (Spool-authored)
User message rendered from src/server/services/prompts/user_message.jinja with analytics summary (stats, anomalies from basic + advanced analytics)
Stores per-recommendation rows in new ai_recommendations table (add SQLAlchemy model)
Updates analysis_history row: status=completed, model={actual Ollama model}, processing_time_ms=real, result_summary=JSON summary
Ollama unreachable: returns 503 with {'detail': 'Ollama unavailable'}, analysis_history status=failed, no crash
Ollama HTTP error: returns 502, analysis_history status=failed
drive_id with no rows in time window: returns 200 with empty recommendations[] and a clear message
Invalid drive_id: returns 404
verification: pytest tests/server/test_analyze.py -v (US-147 compat still passes for stub path if any remains, else verify stub is removed cleanly)
invariants: Response shape from US-147 remains intact — existing Pi-side code must not break
No changes to src/server/analytics/ (AI endpoint consumes analytics output, does not reimplement it)
Ollama calls go through src/server/ai/ollama.py — do not duplicate httpx boilerplate in analyze.py
stopConditions: If src/server/services/prompts/ is empty at story start (Spool templates not yet delivered), STOP and file BL- against Spool. Do not invent tuning prompt content — Rule 7 (no fabricated data).
If ai_recommendations table schema is ambiguous from spec, STOP and propose a schema in the pre-flight audit

stories.title: Auto-analysis on drive receipt
stories.intent: When a sync payload contains a drive_end event, automatically kick off AI analysis for that drive without waiting for a manual /analyze call. Non-blocking — the sync response returns as soon as the data is committed; the AI task runs in the background.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
After successful /sync, if connection_log rows contain event_type=drive_end, the server enqueues analysis via asyncio.create_task()
/sync response returns as soon as the database transaction commits — analysis runs after, does NOT block the HTTP response
Sync response body: autoAnalysisTriggered=true when analysis was enqueued; autoAnalysisTriggered=false otherwise
If Ollama is unavailable at enqueue time: sync still 200, autoAnalysisTriggered=false, WARNING logged
If analysis itself fails after enqueue: ERROR logged with drive_id, sync success unchanged (already returned)
Analysis uses the same service as POST /analyze (src/server/services/analysis.py) — no duplication
Drive boundaries are extracted from the synced connection_log (device_id + drive_start/drive_end pair)
invariants: Sync response time is NOT gated on Ollama latency — analysis is fully async
US-CMP-004 sync contract unchanged except for the new autoAnalysisTriggered field
stopConditions: If US-CMP-005 not passing, STOP — this story is BLOCKED

stories.title: AI-enhanced CLI reports
stories.intent: When AI analysis exists for a drive, the scripts/report.py --drive report should surface the ranked recommendations alongside the statistics. No AI: section is simply omitted, matching the crawl/walk behavior today.
acceptance: Produce pre-flight audit listing files to touch, unknowns, and assumptions before any code change
scripts/report.py --drive N renders the existing statistics + comparison sections AND, when analysis_history has completed rows for that drive, an 'AI Analysis' section
AI Analysis section shows: model name, processing time, ranked recommendations (rank, category text, recommendation text, confidence)
Baseline Status sub-section shows real-baseline calibration state if US-162 baselines exist (e.g. 'Calibrated on N real drives' OR 'Using simulated baselines')
When no AI analysis exists for the drive, the AI Analysis section is simply omitted — no 'No analysis yet' placeholder that clutters the output
Layout matches the example in server spec §3.6
Output renders cleanly in a standard 80-column terminal
Existing --drive, --trends, --last behaviors are unchanged for drives with no AI analysis
invariants: Zero changes to src/server/ — this is a consumer of AI data, not a producer
No regressions in data/regression/expected/*.txt for drives without AI analysis
stopConditions: If US-CMP-005 not passing (no AI data exists), this story is BLOCKED for the AI section — the Baseline Status sub-section from US-162 CAN ship independently if the CIO wants a partial story. Flag in the pre-flight audit.
